summary: Unmarried men have substantially higher rates of accidental death compared to unmarried women, indicating a strong gender difference in mortality linked to marital status; while cancer data are incomplete, the pronounced disparity in accident-related fatalities supports overall variation by gender. $\boxed{\text{A}}$
